headline: Bumiputera Status Denied to 34,000 Orang Asli Despite Constitutional Inclusion
context: Despite Article 153 explicitly including Orang Asli as 'natives of Peninsular Malaysia,' approximately 34,000 Orang Asli report being denied Bumiputera programme access due to administrative classification.

cards.sub: 34,000 report being denied Bumiputera programme access. Administrative systems treat them as a separate category.

cards.big: JAKOA classifies Orang Asli under 18 sub-ethnic codes.
cards.sub: Some codes are not recognised by MARA, TEKUN, or state Bumiputera programme systems.

cards.big: Orang Asli MARA loan approval rate is 12% versus 34% for Malay applicants.
cards.sub: Both are Bumiputera. The approval gap is 22 percentage points.
The constitution recognises both as beneficiaries. Administrative practice prioritises one.

cards.big: Constitutionally Bumiputera, administratively excluded. The gap between law and practice is 34,000 people wide.
If Orang Asli are Bumiputera under Article 153, they must receive identical programme access. Administrative systems that create sub-categories within a constitutional category are unlawful.
